tier: APFS ~2.9-7.8 TiB metadata-overflow dead zone: multi-block spaceman FORMAT + in-place COW commit
host: Local WSL2 archlinux (qemu-system-x86_64 -enable-kvm, macOS Sequoia recovery, apfs_kext 2332.101.1) with a sparse 4 TiB image passed through
change: Closed the ~2.9-7.8 TiB FORMAT 'dead zone'. In this band the spaceman's inline cib-address array overflows one 4096-byte block, but Apple forbids the CAB tier for cib_count <= 507, so the writer previously failed closed. The fix matches Apple/mkapfs: the spaceman ephemeral object spans more than one block (spaceman_size = roundup(cibArrayOffset + cib_count*8, blockSize)) and the inline array spills into the second block. spacemanBlockSpan + apfsFormatEphemeralLayout compute the span and shift the genesis + live checkpoint-data ephemerals (reaper, the two free-queue trees) after each spaceman by spacemanBlocks-1; buildSpacemanBlock emits the multi-block object (object header + fletcher64 cover the whole object); the live checkpoint map records cpm_size = spacemanBlocks * blockSize and the nx_superblock data-ring (data_index/len/next) accounts for the extra blocks. For a single-block spaceman everything reduces to the certified constants (9/10/11/12/13/14, data index 2 / len 4), so all existing tiers stay byte-identical. The writer also marks the created image file sparse (markFileSparse via FSCTL_SET_SPARSE on Windows; POSIX ftruncate is already sparse) so a multi-TiB container materializes only its written metadata.
scenario: S.A.K. format-image of a 4 TiB container (1073741824 blocks -> 32768 chunks -> 261 cib_count, cab_count 0) named DDZ4T. 261 cibs overflow the inline array, so the spaceman spans 2 blocks.
linux_apfsck: apfsck -cw clean (exit 0, no findings) on the S.A.K.-formatted 4 TiB container. Cross-checked: apfsprogs mkapfs also produces an apfsck-clean multi-block spaceman at 4 TiB.

apple_fsck_apfs.kernel_mount_read: the macOS kernel auto-mounted DDZ4T; diskutil info /Volumes/DDZ4T reported APFS Container disk1, APFS Physical Store disk0, Container Free Space 4.4 TB, Allocation Block Size 4096
apple_fsck_apfs.kernel_continuation: the volume superblock records 'formatted by S.A.K. Utility APFS writer and last modified by apfs_kext (2332.101.1)'; the kernel mounted read-write and continued the checkpoint to transaction ID 4
apple_fsck_apfs.container_verdict: The container /dev/disk1 appears to be OK.
apple_fsck_apfs.volume_verdict: The volume /dev/rdisk1s1 with UUID 412613A0-40F1-48DC-9722-56CD6D8C3139 appears to be OK.
phases_clean: space manager (the multi-block spaceman is accepted)
apple_fsck_apfs.evidence: apfs-deadzone-fsck-clean.png (fsck verdict + kernel continuation), apfs-deadzone-kernel-mount.png (diskutil info showing the auto-mounted 4 TiB DDZ4T)

in_place_commit.change: Extended the in-place COW commit to the multi-block spaceman. reemitCheckpointEphemerals now reads each ephemeral at its checkpoint-map cpm_size and re-emits all its blocks (the spaceman spans two in the dead zone), advancing the data-ring offset by the object's block span; checkpointDataBlockSpan drives the nx_superblock data-ring length and the ring-wrap check. The spaceman's mutations (cib_addr re-point, free counts, free queues) live in block 0, so the second block (the cib-array tail) is preserved verbatim. The copyToScratchImage path now copies sparsely (copyFileSparse: FSCTL_QUERY_ALLOCATED_RANGES on Windows, SEEK_DATA/SEEK_HOLE on POSIX) so a 4 TiB image-only commit materializes only its metadata; the single-block fail-closed guard was removed.
in_place_commit.scenario: commit-image-file-insert dz.txt='dead-zone-commit-payload' into the 4 TiB DDZC container (writer xid 2 -> 3); the scratch copied in seconds with no extra disk use.
in_place_commit.linux_apfsck: apfsck -cw on the mutated container reports only the pre-existing benign 'Data stream xfield: wrong flags.' note (no spaceman, checkpoint, or space-manager error).
in_place_commit.kernel_mount_read: the macOS kernel auto-mounted DDZC read-write and cat /Volumes/DDZC/dz.txt returned 'dead-zone-commit-payload' (the file the multi-block-spaceman commit inserted)
in_place_commit.kernel_continuation: writer emitted xid 3; the kernel mounted read-write and continued the checkpoint to transaction ID 6
in_place_commit.container_verdict: The container /dev/disk1 appears to be OK.
in_place_commit.volume_verdict: The volume /dev/rdisk1s1 with UUID 66ED9FF6-9C5A-4141-A149-52F91D865D09 appears to be OK; space manager phase clean.
in_place_commit.evidence: apfs-deadzone-commit-fsck-clean.png (kernel cat of the inserted file + fsck verdict at xid 6)

note: FORMAT and in-place COW commit for the dead zone are now both Apple-certified, so the writer supports the full single-CIB -> multi-CIB -> metadata-overflow -> CAB range with no fail-closed band below 32 TiB for either format or in-place mutation. Unit test apfsWriter_formatsMetadataOverflowDeadZoneMultiBlockSpaceman asserts the 4 TiB spaceman cpm_size is two blocks; the existing in-place commit suite covers the single-block path (the multi-block path is the same loop with span > 1).
